name: ARILSetPDUTimingEvent
title: ARILSetPDUTimingEvent
syntax: long ARILSetPDUTimingEvent (dbMsg dbMessage, long TrueOrFalse, long enable, long repetitionPeriod, long repetitionCount, long debounceDelay, long disturbanceCount, long flags);
description: Overrides the defined event-timing from the database. Possibly two timings (False and True) exist in parallel and are selected upon the current transmission mode of the PDU.
Only when an enabled event-timing exists, the PDU can be transmitted regardless of any cyclic timing if any transfer property of a signal or signal group is firing/triggering.

parameters.name: dbMessage
parameters.description: The symbolic name of a PDU in the database.

parameters.name: TrueOrFalse
parameters.description: 0: denotes the False timing 1: denotes the True timing 3: denotes the True and the False timing

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: Enables (1) or disables (0) the appropriate event timing.

parameters.name: repetitionPeriod
parameters.description: Defines the period in [ms] for the possible repeated transmissions (only valid and used, when repetitionCount > 0).

parameters.name: repetitionCount
parameters.description: Defines the amount of repeated transmissions after the event occurred (0 means only one transmission without any repetition).

parameters.name: debounceDelay
parameters.description: Defines the minimal distance in [ms] of any two transmissions of this PDU (bandwidth control). 0 means that PDU can be transmitted as fast as possible and possibly produces bursts.

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
